headline: Walmart CONFIRMED at 28.9 Developing — standard rotation assessment. WARN Act filings: 2,302 workers affected across 5 states (latest through May 1, 2026). Ongoing NLRB cases (250+ since 2001), worker surveillance technology concerns, unreasonable quota allegations per labor reports. No major new discrete event in May 11-12 window. Cumulative labor-welfare signals already reflected in published composite. Confirm at 28.9 Developing.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.25 unchanged. WARN Act filings provide some structural detection mechanism via state notification.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.25 unchanged. WARN compliance is minimum legal threshold.

key_evidence: WARN Act filings: 2,302 workers affected across 5 states (latest through May 1, 2026).
Worker surveillance technology concerns documented in labor reports.
Unreasonable quota allegations per labor reports.
Responsible-recruitment commitment progress (2026 target year for supplier labor practice standards) — forward-watch indicator.

evidence.finding: 2,302 workers affected across 5 states through May 1, 2026.

evidence.source: WNY Labor Today — Working at a Walmart deli analysis

negativeSignals: WARN filings; NLRB case accumulation; surveillance concerns; quota allegations.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
triage_note: Triage 2026-07-21: superseded by newer assessment 2026-06-10. Not applied; current published score is authoritative.
